decks.description: This deck is built entirely around the 'attacks alone' mechanic, rewarding you for sending a single creature into combat rather than swinging wide. Peggy Carter, Secret Agent grants indestructible to a lone attacker, Agent 13 Sharon Carter turns that same attack into a Clue for card draw, Agents of S.H.I.E.L.D. and Strategic Intervention both pump the attacker and can tap down blockers, and Nick Fury, Spymaster turns every solo swing into a card plus a free tapped-and-attacking creature cheat from hand. Quake, Agent of S.H.I.E.L.D. adds a secondary layer of tempo disruption, tapping down a creature or land whenever you cast a noncreature spell, which combines nicely with the deck's density of removal/utility instants like Helicarrier Strike and Web Up. The overall plan is to establish one resilient, growing threat, protect it with indestructible effects, and let the alone-attack triggers snowball into card advantage and near-unblockable damage, while S.H.I.E.L.D. Helicarrier and Borough Backup provide token backup for board stalls or crewing.
tips: Attack with exactly one creature
Use Peggy Carter for indestructible
Chain Nick Fury for extra bodies
Quake taps blockers off noncreature spells
Protect your lone attacker first

decks.description: Caretakers is a lifegain payoff deck where every point of life you gain snowballs into bigger creatures and card advantage. MJ, Rising Star puts a +1/+1 counter on herself whenever you gain life, while Doctor Strange, Surgeon doubles all life gained, turning even small triggers like Valkyrior Skyrider's 4 life or Crowd of True Believers' lifelink pump into huge swings, and once you're 10 life above your starting total his ability gives your whole team +2/+2 and vigilance every combat. Night Nurse and Doctor Jane Foster both recur permanents from the graveyard, with Jane Foster specifically upgrading to a full battlefield return if you've gained life that turn, rewarding sequencing your lifegain spells before her ETB. The deck's core loop is simple: gain life to fuel Doctor Strange's doubling and MJ's growth, use Take Up the Shield and Infinity Formula to protect and further buff your threats, and grind out value through recursion.
tips: Sequence lifegain before Doctor Strange
Gain life before casting Jane Foster
Let MJ snowball off small lifegain
Protect threats with Take Up the Shield
Play Doctor Strange first for max doubling

decks.description: Counterargument is a +1/+1 counter synergy deck that spreads small buffs across the board and then leverages ward and doubling effects to protect and explode its board state. Matt Murdock, Justice Seeker gives every counter-bearing creature ward {1}, making your team hard to remove once buffed, while She-Hulk, Attorney-at-Law's power-up ability doubles every counter on your board for a massive one-time swing. Political Triumph quietly accrues plan counters off every creature ETB, then cashes in for a card and a counter on your whole team, and Costume Closet stores extra counters to redistribute later, especially useful with Selfless Police Captain's counters transferring elsewhere when it dies. The deck plays a steady value game, using ETB creatures like Daily Bugle Reporters and Foggy Nelson to place counters and protect key threats with hexproof, before going over the top with She-Hulk's doubling to close out the game.
tips: Spread counters early
Save counters for She-Hulk doubling
Ward protects countered creatures
Use Political Triumph for card draw
Recycle counters with Costume Closet

decks.description: Wakanda is a go-wide token deck that fills the board with 1/1 Soldiers and Hero tokens, then leverages payoffs that scale with creature count and token synergies. Wakandan Shield Guard, Okoye, and Borough Backup all pump out extra bodies, while Okoye's static ability gives all attacking creature tokens first strike, making combat very favorable once the board is wide. Ultimate Alliance deals damage equal to your total creature count, turning a wide board into direct removal, and Black Panther, Most Dangerous's power-up buffs the whole team by +2/+2 for a big alpha strike. Shuri and Secure Detention add flexible value and removal, while Vibranium Energy Daggers and Panther Pounce push through extra damage on key attackers; the overall plan is simply to flood the board cheaply and use the count-based payoffs to finish the game.
tips: Flood board with token generators
Okoye gives tokens first strike
Ultimate Alliance scales with board size
Pop Black Panther power-up for alpha strike
Use Secure Detention on biggest blocker

decks.description: Battalion is built around attacking with three or more creatures at once to trigger its namesake keyword on several key creatures. General Thunderbolt Ross, Falcon, and Captain America, Skybound all have Battalion abilities that trigger simultaneously when you swing wide with at least three attackers, granting team-wide +1/+0, a growing Falcon plus scry, and indestructible +1/+1 counters across the whole attacking team respectively. War Machine and Wild Pack Squad add tempo by tapping down blockers before combat, clearing the way for your battalion swing, while U.S.Agent's free Sturdy Shield equipment and The Howling Commandos' team pump provide extra buffs to push through lethal. The deck's plan is to hold back until you can assemble a three-plus creature attack, then unleash Captain America's indestructible counters combined with Thunderbolt Ross's damage boost to blow out combat math in your favor.
tips: Attack with three or more creatures
Stack Battalion triggers together
Tap blockers with War Machine first
Captain America gives indestructible counters
Use Howling Commandos to push lethal

decks.description: Equipped is a classic Voltron-adjacent Equipment deck that stacks gear onto a handful of key creatures to build one or two overwhelming threats. Captain America, Liberator tutors an Equipment onto the battlefield for free and creates a Soldier token per Equipment attached whenever he attacks, synergizing directly with Origin of Captain America's Chapter II Sturdy Shield token and U.S.Agent's own free Sturdy Shield, letting you accumulate multiple Equipment cheaply. Captain America's Shield turns any creature into a huge 0/8+ wall-breaker with vigilance and a tap effect on attack, while Bucky Barnes digs for more Equipment, Hero, or Soldier cards when he dies. Peggy Carter and Agent 13 reward attacking alone with indestructible and card draw, so the deck can pivot between a wide Soldier-token plan off Equipment triggers and a single fortified attacker protected by indestructible effects like Take Up the Shield.
tips: Equip Captain America Liberator for tokens
Stack multiple Equipment on one attacker
Attack alone with Peggy Carter up
Use Bucky Barnes death trigger to dig
Protect key creature with Take Up the Shield

decks.description: Precise is a targeted-spell counters deck where creatures like Colleen Wing, Street Samurai, Hawkeye, Bowslinger, and Mockingbird, Ace Agent all grow permanently whenever you cast a spell that targets a creature you control, turning combat tricks and utility spells into long-term stat growth. Stunning Shot doubles as both a buff (two counters) and removal (stun a blocker), directly feeding these targeting triggers while also disabling an opponent's creature, and Panther Pounce similarly targets your own creature for value while also untapping and granting flying. Because so many of your creatures reward you for targeting your own side, even simple tricks like Heroic Teamwork or Sudden Strike generate lasting value by growing Colleen Wing, Hawkeye, or Mockingbird every time they're cast, letting an unassuming board slowly turn into several oversized evasive or double-striking threats.
tips: Target your own creatures often
Grow Colleen Wing and Hawkeye with tricks
Use Stunning Shot for buff and removal
Mockingbird gets huge with double strike
Sequence spells for max counter triggers

decks.description: Web-Slinging leans into the Spider-tribal 'web-slinging' mechanic, where creatures like Spider-Man, Web-Slinger, Spider-UK, and Amazing Spider-Girl can be cast cheaply by bouncing a tapped creature back to hand, letting you replay ETB triggers repeatedly. Aunt May gains you life on every creature ETB and adds a +1/+1 counter specifically to Spiders, while Spider-UK's end step trigger draws a card and gains 2 life whenever two or more creatures entered that turn, which is easy to hit since web-slinging naturally causes you to re-enter a bounced creature alongside a new spell. Spider-Mobile scales its combat stats with your Spider count, rewarding the tribal build-up, and Thwip! gives an extra life-gain boost specifically when targeting a Spider. The overall gameplan is to chain cheap web-slinging casts each turn to re-trigger ETBs like Virtuous Variant's counter or Flash Thompson's tap/untap modes, flooding the board with value while accumulating life and cards along the way.
tips: Bounce tapped creatures to recast cheaply
Chain ETBs for Spider-UK's card draw
Aunt May rewards flooding the board
Spider-Mobile scales with Spider count
Replay Virtuous Variant for repeat counters

decks.description: Marvelous is built around blink and exile effects that reset creatures for repeatable ETB value, alongside a lifegain-to-damage payoff in Captain Marvel, Shooting Star. Photon, Lady of Light exiles and returns another creature you control whenever she attacks, letting you replay ETB effects like Ms. Marvel's pump-and-draw trigger or Wakandan Shield Guard's token creation every combat, while Quantum Entanglement offers a repeatable flash blink each end step for the same purpose. Captain Marvel herself exiles a creature on ETB or attack for a life-swing effect, gaining her controller life whenever any creature (other than herself) is exiled from the battlefield, which combos directly with Photon's and Quantum Entanglement's blink triggers to generate incidental lifegain on top of the ETB replay value. Marvel Boy, Noh-Varr grows off every creature entering or power-up activation, capping off a deck that wants to chain enter-the-battlefield triggers as often as possible while incidentally gaining life along the way.
tips: Blink creatures with Photon or Quantum Entanglement
Captain Marvel gains life off any exile
Replay Ms. Marvel and Shield Guard ETBs
Grow Marvel Boy off every ETB
Time blinks around your attack step

decks.description: Squadron is a Hero-tribal deck that showers its creatures with small combat buffs and scales up through the 'attacks alone' exalted mechanic. Zarda, the Power Princess grants every other Hero you control exalted, so any lone-attacking Hero gets a cumulative +1/+1 for each instance, and Nighthawk, Dark Defender adds another layer by pumping a creature whenever any Hero enters, meaning simply deploying more Heroes keeps buffing your attackers even outside combat. Doctor Spectrum offers modal flexibility to make a defensive wall, buff the team, or blow up an enchantment, while Hyperion, Supreme Hero's damage-prevention shield keeps your Heroes alive through combat and burn. Hyperion's Atomic Vision's behold mechanic turns any Hero into a scry-attached removal spell, and the deck closes games by isolating a single well-buffed Hero attacker empowered by Zarda's exalted stack and Nighthawk's incidental pumps.
tips: Stack exalted for lone attackers
Deploy Heroes to trigger Nighthawk pumps
Behold a Hero for Atomic Vision scry
Use Doctor Spectrum flexibly
Protect Heroes with Hyperion's damage shield

decks.description: This deck is built around the Teamwork mechanic, where tapping down creatures pays additional costs for powerful spells like We Say Thee Nay!, Timeline Inquiry, Atlantis Attacks, and Quantum Reduction, all of which get stronger or gain flash when the teamwork cost is met. Alongside this, Viv Vision, Machine Man Model X-51, and TVA Bureaucrat all reward casting noncreature spells with pumps, unblockability, or card draw, meaning the deck's payoff creatures grow every time you fire off one of its many teamwork or countermagic spells. Vision, Spectral Synthezoid lets you cast a free noncreature or Robot spell every turn, which combines directly with Echo, Perceptive Prodigy's ability to copy an activated or triggered ability, letting you double up value from ETBs and power-ups. Virtual Assistant also converts every teamwork-cast spell into a flying Robot token, so the deck naturally builds a small air force while controlling the board with bounce and counters.
tips: Tap creatures for teamwork bonuses
Chain noncreature spells for value
Use Vision's free spell each turn
Copy triggers with Echo
Hold up countermagic like We Say Thee Nay!

decks.description: This is a mono-blue/black-leaning connive deck (splash of black via Villainous Hideout) where nearly every creature and spell triggers connive to filter draws into +1/+1 counters, snowballing card advantage into bigger threats. Doc Ock's Henchmen, Tiger Shark Abyssal Hunter, and Leader Super-Genius all connive on ETB or attack, while Leader Super-Genius's static ability turns every connive trigger into a free extra card draw before the discard, effectively doubling the value of every connive across the whole deck. Doc Ock, Sinister Scientist scales into an 8/8 once the graveyard (fed by all that discarding) hits eight cards, and gains hexproof as long as you control another Villain, making him very hard to remove once the engine gets going. The deck's removal (Trickster's Stratagem, Frozen in Ice equivalents like Quantum Reduction elsewhere) is light here, so it wins mostly by out-carding opponents and building an unkillable threat.
tips: Leader doubles connive draws
Feed graveyard for Doc Ock
Villain synergy grants hexproof
Discard lands, keep threats
Attack with Tiger Shark repeatedly

decks.description: This is a pure card-draw value deck where nearly every creature either draws a card outright or grows off drawing extra cards, all supported by Reed Richards, Smartest Man's ability to turn your first non-draw-step draw each turn into four cards instead. That massive card advantage directly fuels Blue Marvel and Kid Loki, both of which put a +1/+1 counter on themselves whenever you draw your second card each turn, so Reed Richards' quadrupled draw effectively guarantees those counters trigger constantly. Beast, Erudite Aerialist and Ant-Man both convert combat damage into more cards, while Brawn, Amadeus Cho and Bold Biochemist let you dump your now-bloated hand into raw stats via their power-up abilities. The deck doesn't need much removal since it simply out-draws and out-values opponents until its power-up threats are too big to answer.
tips: Reed Richards multiplies draws
Trigger second-draw counters
Dump hand into Brawn
Attack to refuel via Beast/Ant-Man
Power-up late for huge stats

decks.description: This is an artifact-tempo deck that leans on bounce and tap effects to control the board while Iron Man, Bleeding Edge and Iron Lad, Young Avenger make every artifact and noncreature spell more efficient or explosive. Iron Man's ability to copy any artifact spell you cast pairs directly with Iron Suitcase, Collector's Case, and Futurist Forge, turning a single cast into two ETB triggers (double scry, double stun-tap, or double card draw), while Iron Lad's cost reduction on noncreature spells makes casting these artifacts and spells like I Am Iron Man cheaper and faster. Origin of Iron Man's saga chain digs for a big artifact drop for free by chapter III, and Happy Hogan, Bodyguard combined with Rescue, Pepper Potts gives repeatable bounce/tuck effects to buy tempo. Overall the deck grinds out card advantage and board control through cheap artifacts and repeatable bounce rather than pure aggression.
tips: Copy artifacts with Iron Man
Chain Iron Lad's cost reduction
Stun lock with Collector's Case
Free artifact via Origin saga
Bounce blockers before attacking

decks.description: This is a Merfolk tribal deck that builds around connive and the 'draw your second card each turn' trigger to grow a team of counters-matter creatures. Attuma, Atlantean Warlord anthems other Merfolk and draws a card whenever Merfolk attack, while Namor, Scourge of the Seas grants flying to any Merfolk with a +1/+1 counter, meaning creatures pumped by Atlantean Cavalry's or Daughter of the Deep's second-draw triggers become evasive threats. Namora, the Sea Queen's power-up creates more Merfolk tokens to fuel Attuma's attack trigger, and Atlantean Skirmisher and Namor's connive abilities keep converting extra cards into more counters. Atlantis Attacks and Quantum Reduction (both Teamwork spells) let you tap the wide Merfolk board to either build a huge Leviathan/bounce two permanents or shut down an opposing threat, rewarding the deck's go-wide, counter-laden game plan.
tips: Pump counters via second draws
Namor grants counter-flyers
Attuma draws on Merfolk attacks
Tap team for teamwork spells
Namora tokens fuel the swarm

decks.description: This deck's core plan is repeatedly bouncing or blinking your own permanents to reuse ETB triggers and grow value creatures. Silver Surfer, Cosmic Voyager exiles any number of your permanents and returns them at end of turn, re-triggering ETBs like S.H.I.E.L.D. Deployment Drone's token creation or Shipwreck Patrol's stun-tap, while Spaceshift does something similar to a single artifact or creature, adding a +1/+1 counter in the process. Justice, Vance Astrovik and Imperial Cosmographer both reward permanents leaving and returning to the battlefield with +1/+1 counters, so bouncing your own stuff with Shi'ar Soldier or Silver Surfer directly feeds their growth. Victor Timely, Wily Tycoon lets you recast a cheap instant/sorcery/artifact from the graveyard for free, effectively giving you a second use out of spells like Collector's Case or We Say Thee Nay!, rounding out a deck that thrives on reusing its own effects rather than committing new cards to the board.
tips: Blink with Silver Surfer for value
Rebounce to trigger Justice
Reuse ETBs each turn
Recast spells with Victor Timely
Spaceshift resets and pumps

decks.description: This deck centers on power-up abilities and size-shrinking effects to close games, using Wasp, Shrinking Savior's attack trigger to shrink an opposing blocker to -3/-0 and then draw a card for every creature on the battlefield with negative power, which combos directly with Depower's -4/-0 effect and Quantum Reduction's -5/-0 aura to push creatures below zero power and refill your hand. Ant-Man, Reformed Rogue grows and becomes unblockable off blue spells while gaining trample off green spells, matching this deck's mix of blue tempo and green pump effects like Robotics Mastery and Super Suit. Aerial Doombot and Bold Biochemist both have big late-game power-up modes that turn any flooded hand into a game-ending threat or fresh cards, while Giant-Sized Flying Ant's flexible tap/untap ETB provides combat trickery to either push through damage or blank a blocker.
tips: Shrink creatures below zero power
Wasp draws off negative power
Stack Depower and Quantum Reduction
Ant-Man rewards blue/green spells
Power-up late for big payoff

decks.description: This is a flying-tribal tempo deck where Vulture, Feathered Fiend rewards any flier connecting with combat damage by putting a +1/+1 counter on every flier that hit and drawing a card for each, snowballing hard once multiple fliers like Aerial Doombot, Namora, S.H.I.E.L.D. Deployment Drone, and Falcon's token Redwing are attacking together. Falcon, Winged Wonder adds another evasive body in Redwing, whose surveil-on-attack trigger smooths draws, while Falcon's Wing Harness can retrofit a ground creature with flying and ward to join the air assault and feed Vulture's trigger even further. Flying Drone's loot ability gets cheaper whenever another flier enters that turn, meaning a turn where you deploy multiple fliers also lets you filter your hand efficiently, and bounce spells like Whoosh! and I Am Iron Man help clear blockers or set up alpha strikes.
tips: Swing wide with fliers
Vulture draws on flier damage
Equip Falcon's Wing Harness to grounders
Flying Drone loots cheap after fliers ETB
Bounce blockers before attacking

decks.description: This deck is a time-manipulation control shell that leverages extra draws and extra turns to overwhelm opponents. Immortus, Master of Eternity taps for mana equal to cards drawn that turn just to cast noncreature spells, synergizing with a deck full of cheap draw spells like Think Twice, Timeline Inquiry, and Bold Biochemist's power-up, letting you chain several noncreature spells off one big draw turn; his own power-up ability then wheels everyone's hand and graveyard for a fresh seven, resetting the game state in your favor. Pharaoh Rama-Tut connives off every noncreature spell you cast, meaning the deck's dense noncreature spell count directly grows him into a real threat while digging further. Time Warp for an extra turn and Multiversal Recruitment's flashback to copy your best creature both let this value-heavy deck close out games once its card advantage has taken over, with Victor Timely, Wily Tycoon providing extra reach by recasting a cheap spell for free from the graveyard.
tips: Immortus taps off draws
Chain cheap draw spells
Rama-Tut grows off spells
Time Warp to close games
Recur spells with Victor Timely

decks.description: This deck's whole identity is copying creatures to generate value and confuse combat math. Loki, Lord of Misrule can turn your entire board into copies of your best creature each turn, The Clone Saga's second chapter copies the next creature you cast that turn, and Multiversal Recruitment makes a nonlegendary token copy of any creature you control, all feeding into a shell where Impossible Man can also become a copy of any permanent at instant speed for a surprise blocker or attacker. Living Lies of Loki scales with the number of Illusions you control and refunds a card when it dies, meshing with the deck's token-copy plan since token copies of Illusions boost its power further. Kid Loki rewards any turn you place +1/+1 counters with hexproof, protecting your best copied threat, while We Say Thee Nay!, Quantum Reduction, and Trickster's Stratagem provide interaction to protect your combo pieces or answer opposing threats while your copy engine takes over the game.
tips: Copy your best creature
Loki turns board into clones
Clone Saga doubles a cast creature
Living Lies scales with Illusions
Protect key threats with hexproof

decks.description: This deck is built around the 'attacks alone' payoff subtheme mixed with a Villain-tribal token engine. Bob, Reluctant HYDRA Agent bounces himself after attacking alone to drain 2 life, and Viper, Cruel Conspirator pumps or grants deathtouch/lifelink to any lone attacker, making solo swings surprisingly resilient and punishing. Alongside that, Baron Strucker, HYDRA Overlord discounts Villain spells and lets newly-entering Villains connive once per turn, feeding both card selection and +1/+1 counters, while HYDRA Troopers and Arnim Zola, Bio-Fanatic turn a stocked graveyard into a stream of 2/1 menace Villain tokens. Crossbones, Malicious Mercenary converts each new Villain entering into a permanent counter and 2 damage to the opponent, so the token generators (HYDRA Troopers, Arnim Zola, HYDRA Disintegrator) directly power him up. The deck grinds out value through removal (Dark Deed, Cruel Alliance) and recursion (Infernal Rebirth) while chipping away at life totals with solo attackers and a wide army of Villain tokens.
tips: Attack alone with Bob for repeated drain
Pump lone attackers with Viper
Fill graveyard to fuel Arnim Zola tokens
Let Crossbones snowball off new Villains
Use Baron Strucker to discount and connive

decks.description: Masters of Evil is a Villain-tribal aggro-value deck that rewards flooding the board with Villains and attacking as a group, in contrast to the attack-alone subtheme seen elsewhere in the set. The Masters of Evil enchantment itself gives every other Villain +2/+1, turning modest bodies into serious threats, while Crimson Cowl, Master of Evil creates a 2/1 menace Villain token whenever nontoken Villains attack together, continually replenishing the board. Yellowjacket, Heartless Marauder gets bigger and gains lifelink every time another Villain enters, so token generators like Crimson Cowl and Tiger Shark's connive ability keep compounding its value. Villainous Syndication mills for value and can eventually reanimate a creature from the graveyard, tying into the deck's incidental self-mill from Tiger Shark and Klaw. The overall plan is to deploy Villains, pump the team with the anthem, swing wide, and use targeted removal like Hour of Defeat and Cruel Alliance to clear blockers.
tips: Play Masters of Evil early for the anthem
Attack with multiple Villains to trigger Crimson Cowl
Chain Villain ETBs to pump Yellowjacket
Use Tiger Shark connive for card selection
Save Cruel Alliance for a key blocker

decks.description: Returned is a graveyard-recursion deck centered on repeatedly bringing creatures back from the dead and profiting each time one leaves the yard. Undead Hand Ninja and Grim Reaper's Scythe both trigger whenever creature cards leave your graveyard, draining the opponent or making a 2/2 Zombie token respectively, so effects like Infernal Rebirth, Too Evil to Stay Dead, and Unliving Legionnaire's power-up ability that return creatures from the graveyard to hand or battlefield double as engines for those triggers. Grim Reaper, Lethal Legionnaire lets you reanimate a creature straight into combat as an attacker for extra tempo, while Grim Reaper's Scythe can later sacrifice two creatures to bring back something with a finality counter. Ultimo, Civilization's End and Minion Missile provide sacrifice-based removal that also feeds your own graveyard fuel, and Arnim Zola turns a stocked graveyard into a steady stream of menace tokens, letting the deck grind through removal and reanimation loops while opponents struggle to keep up with recurring threats.
tips: Fill graveyard early with mill effects
Return creatures to trigger Undead Hand Ninja
Reanimate into combat with Grim Reaper
Sac fodder to Scythe for reanimation
Use Ultimo/Minion Missile as sac-removal

decks.description: Street Level Villains is a sacrifice-value deck that turns dying creatures and artifacts into card draw, Treasures, and extra bodies. Kingpin, Wilson Fisk creates two Treasure tokens whenever you sacrifice him or another creature, and those Treasures can immediately fuel Deadly Dispute, which itself demands a sacrifice to draw two cards and make another Treasure, creating a self-sustaining loop of sacrifice fodder and mana. Hammerhead, Maggia Boss and Kingpin's Enforcers both want fodder to sacrifice for counters or card draw, while Madame Masque's connive and 'second card drawn' trigger churns out 2/1 menace Villain tokens to keep feeding the sacrifice engine. Count Nefaria becomes drastically cheaper once you've sacrificed a permanent that turn, letting the deck cast a large flying threat for a steep discount off the back of all the sacrificing, and Criminal Enterprise turns dying Villains into direct damage and life gain.
tips: Chain sacrifices into Treasures
Feed Deadly Dispute with fodder
Sac a permanent before casting Nefaria
Let Madame Masque tokens refill board
Use Kingpin sac trigger for ramp

decks.description: Armed is an Equipment-matters deck that wants to stack multiple Equipment onto its threats for compounding bonuses and payoffs. Whiplash, Vengeful Engineer drains the opponent equal to the number of Equipment attached to him when he attacks, so Swordsman, Sharp Scoundrel's ability to auto-attach an Equipment whenever another Villain enters helps get gear onto Whiplash quickly and cheaply, while also triggering connive on equipped attackers for card selection and counters. Swordsman's Steel rewards having several Equipment already out by drawing a card for each one and scaling its own bonus the same way, making it a late-game payoff for a deck that runs HYDRA Disintegrator, Stolen Stark Tech, and Voltaic Whip. Ronin, Shadow Stalker doubles as a mana source for Equipment costs and can sacrifice an attached Equipment to shrink a blocker, giving the deck some removal even while it's committed to its equipment plan. Armed Assailant and U.S.Agent (who comes with his own built-in Equipment) round out the curve as strong equip targets that get menace or extra stats once geared up.
tips: Stack Equipment on Whiplash for drain
Let Swordsman auto-attach for tempo
Play Swordsman's Steel with Equipment out
Use Ronin mana for equip costs
Sac Equipment via Ronin as removal

decks.description: Lethal is a deathtouch-tribal deck where nearly every creature has deathtouch, turning combat damage into a snowballing win condition. White Widow, Yelena Belova and Black Widow, Deadly Hunter both trigger whenever a deathtouch creature connects with a player, growing White Widow with +1/+1 counters and letting Black Widow draw cards, so any deathtouch creature getting through combat damage fuels both engines simultaneously. Venom, Evil Unleashed can be exiled from the graveyard to grant deathtouch and counters to any creature, extending the theme even to non-deathtouch bodies, while Origin of Black Widow forces a sacrifice, grants blanket deathtouch, and finishes with a graveyard-based life-loss burst. Widow's Bite offers flexible removal or a deathtouch grant with Teamwork to enable trades, and the low-to-the-ground deathtouch creatures make combat math nightmarish for opponents who can't afford to block or take damage.
tips: Attack with deathtouch creatures often
Feed Black Widow/White Widow triggers
Use Venom to spread deathtouch
Force bad blocks with deathtouch
Save Widow's Bite for key combat

decks.description: Mayhem is built around the Mayhem keyword, letting you cast key spells and creatures from the graveyard for a discount if you discarded them the same turn, so the deck actively wants to discard cards rather than treat discard as a downside. Green Goblin, Back for More lets you discard a card at the beginning of combat to make an opponent discard too, and that very act can set up Swarm, Being of Bees, Sadistic Slash, or Rocket-Powered Goblin Glider to later be cast for their cheap Mayhem costs from the graveyard. Rocket-Powered Goblin Glider even attaches itself automatically if cast via Mayhem from the graveyard, giving a free equip. Daily Bugle Newspaper loots every turn to find discard fodder and generates Treasure, while Roxxon Brutes and Alien Symbiosis (which can also be cast from the graveyard by discarding a card) both reward drawing multiple cards or discarding respectively, tying the whole deck's discard-and-recast loop together into a resilient, resource-efficient plan.
tips: Discard cards to set up Mayhem
Loot with Daily Bugle Newspaper
Cast Glider from graveyard for free attach
Use Green Goblin to discard proactively
Recast Sadistic Slash cheaply as removal

decks.description: Thunderbolts is a lifegain-matters deck that converts incidental life gain into card advantage and permanent stat growth. Citizen V, Helmut Zemo puts a +1/+1 counter on every Villain you control whenever you gain life, so lifelink creatures like Songbird and Atlas, or a big burst like Void Helix's 5 life, all translate directly into a bigger board. MACH-1, Swooping Scoundrel surveils once per turn whenever you gain life, smoothing draws off the same lifegain triggers, while Valentina Allegra de Fontaine turns other Villains into Heroes and lets a Hero connive each turn for extra selection and counters. Moonstone, Harsh Mistress lets you recur discarded cards temporarily, pairing with Songbird's and Roxxon Brutes' discard/draw synergies, and Eerie Gravestone plus basic landcycling smooth out the mana while digging for threats, making this a value-oriented lifegain snowball deck rather than a pure aggro list.
tips: Gain life to trigger Citizen V counters
Chain lifegain into MACH-1 surveil
Connive Heroes with Valentina
Recur discards via Moonstone
Use Void Helix as removal and lifegain

decks.description: DOOM is a self-mill and graveyard-value deck culminating in an alternate win condition. Doombot Harbinger and Doom's Servo-Guards both mill you as they enter, filling the graveyard for Doomsday, which searches library and graveyard for five cards to stack on top of your library while costing half your life, setting up a very fast draw-out finish once combined with Doctor Doom, Unrivaled's repeatable card draw that wins the game outright if it empties your library. Dark Ritual accelerates into Doomsday or an early Doctor Doom, while Deadly Dispute and Common Crook turn sacrificed fodder into Treasures and draw, and Doombot Harbinger's death trigger can return a creature card from the graveyard to hand to keep bodies coming. Removal like Doom Blade, Doomfall, and Roxxon Brutes' menace keep the board stable while the mill/draw engine assembles the Doomsday-into-Doctor Doom kill.
tips: Mill early to stock graveyard
Dark Ritual to power out Doomsday
Stack five cards via Doomsday
Draw through library with Doctor Doom
Use Doombot Harbinger to recur bodies

decks.description: Ultron is an artifact-graveyard synergy deck where artifacts dying or being milled directly power up its threats. Ultron's Auxiliary and Ultron the Annihilator both trigger whenever an artifact goes to your graveyard from the battlefield or arrives there from elsewhere, growing Auxiliary with counters and draining opponents via Ultron the Annihilator, so the deck wants a steady stream of expendable Robot Villain artifact tokens and Equipment to feed the graveyard. Ultron the Annihilator and Ultron Drone both create 2/2 Robot Villain artifact tokens on entry/attack or via its power-up ability, giving plenty of sacrificial or trigger fodder, while Robot Domination converts creatures dying into card draw and eventually explodes into three more Robot tokens once it hits three plan counters. Victor Mancha, Runaway and Doombot Harbinger both let you rebuy value from the graveyard (a card to play, or a creature card to hand), and Eerie Gravestone's mill-four sacrifice ability both draws a card and feeds the artifact graveyard trigger simultaneously.
tips: Feed artifacts to graveyard for triggers
Chain Robot tokens for fodder
Crack Eerie Gravestone to mill and draw
Use Victor Mancha to rebuy key cards
Build toward Robot Domination's token burst

decks.description: Boosted is a mono-red ramp-into-power-up deck built around Quinjet Technician's ability to generate double red mana specifically for activating power-up abilities, letting you fire off the deck's many expensive power-up triggers ahead of schedule. Nearly every creature carries a power-up ability that scales it into a threat while triggering a payoff: Marvel Boy, Noh-Varr grows every time any power-up ability resolves or a creature enters, snowballing counters as you chain activations, while Wonder Man, Hollywood Hero lets every power-up ability on your board (including his own) fire an additional time, effectively doubling the value from Extremis Elite's damage ping, Jack of Hearts' card refill, and Abomination's fight ability. The deck's payoff cards like Vision of Love and Heroes' Hangout also reward the discard/sacrifice sub-theme, giving you card advantage to keep casting spells and enabling more power-ups. The plan is to ramp mana via Quinjet Technician, land a few power-up creatures, and then use Wonder Man plus extra mana to double- or even triple-dip on abilities in a single turn for a huge swing.
tips: Use Quinjet Technician's RR only for power-ups
Land Wonder Man to double power-up triggers
Chain power-ups to grow Marvel Boy
Sac artifacts/discard to fuel Vision of Love
Sequence power-ups after creatures enter for max Marvel Boy counters

decks.description: Speedy is a mono-red haste-aggro deck whose entire game plan is to deploy hasty threats and immediately push through unblockable damage before the opponent can stabilize. Quicksilver, Brash Blur can start in play from the opening hand, and once you have extra mana his power-up adds a double strike counter, turning him into a fast clock. The deck's key synergy is between creatures that entered this turn and Whirlwind, Killer Cyclone / Pick Up the Pace / Speed, Young Avenger: Whirlwind makes an opposing creature unable to block whenever a freshly-entered creature attacks, Pick Up the Pace lets you exile and play extra cards off those same attacks, and Speed can make a hasty attacker unblockable for a mana, all built to protect and enable the wave of hasty creatures like Volcanic Villain, The Whizzer, and Living Lightning. Taxi Driver and Super Speed retroactively grant haste to anything that stumbles into play without it, keeping the aggressive tempo consistent turn after turn.
tips: Start Quicksilver from opening hand
Attack every turn with fresh haste creatures
Whirlwind shuts down blockers on entering attackers
Use Taxi Driver/Super Speed to grant haste
Power up Quicksilver for double strike finisher

decks.description: Thor is a mono-red burn/damage-doubling deck anchored by Thor, Asgard's Avenger, whose static ability adds 1 extra damage to any noncombat damage your other sources deal to opponents or their permanents, pumping up every burn spell and pinger in the deck. This turns Lightning Bolt and Lightning Strike into 4-damage spells, makes Sif's Spearmaster's tap ability hit harder, and boosts Origin of Thor's chapter III finisher. Molten Lavamancer converts that same non-combat damage into free 1/1 Elemental tokens once per turn, while Loki Laufeyson can copy your cheap burn spells for extra value, and Asgardian Inspiration recurs itself from the graveyard whenever you ping an opponent. The deck wants to land Thor early, then chain burn spells and tap abilities to snowball incremental damage, tokens, and card advantage simultaneously.
tips: Land Thor early to boost all burn
Use Molten Lavamancer to farm free tokens
Loki copies your best burn spell
Recur Asgardian Inspiration by pinging opponents
Tap Sif's Spearmaster for repeatable reach

decks.description: Young Avengers is a mono-red spells-matter deck where nearly every creature has a trigger off casting noncreature spells, rewarding you for jamming as many instants and sorceries as possible each turn. Iron Lad, Young Avenger discounts those noncreature spells by {1}, making it easier to chain them and trigger Stature (who becomes a 4/4 until end of turn), Patriot (prowess plus a team-wide +1/+0 while equipped), Wiccan (exiling and letting you play the top card of your library), and especially Hulkling, Young Avenger, who becomes a copy of any other target creature you control (turning into a 4/4 flying version of your best creature) every time you cast a noncreature spell. Hawkeye's static ability adds his power to any noncombat damage a source you control deals, so pairing him with burn spells like Lightning Strike or Marvelous Melee, or with Hawkeye's Bow's tap trigger, meaningfully increases damage output. The deck's teamwork spells (Crossover Collaboration, Team Tactics) let you tap down cheap creatures for bonus value like extra card exiles or trample, rewarding a wide, spell-slinging board.
tips: Cast noncreature spells to trigger everyone
Hulkling copies your best creature each turn
Hawkeye boosts all noncombat damage sources
Equip Patriot for team-wide pump
Use teamwork spells for bonus value

decks.description: Fearless is a mono-red self-damage/masochism deck that turns damage dealt to you into card advantage and reach. Stick, Fearless Mentor and Daredevil, Fearless Fighter both trigger whenever a source you control deals damage to you: Stick exiles and lets you play the top card of your library, while Daredevil redirects that same damage straight at an opponent, meaning painlands like Fogwell's Gym or self-damaging cards like Daredevil's Billy Club and Elektra, Femme Fatale double as both card advantage engines and direct burn once Daredevil is on board. Daredevil also exiles a card off his own attack trigger and burns you for its mana value, feeding the same synergy loop. Jessica Jones and Unlucky Witness add further card selection through stun counters and death triggers, while Stegron the Dinosaur Man and Hire a Crew provide combat-oriented pump and tokens to close out games once the card advantage has piled up.
tips: Play Daredevil to redirect self-damage as burn
Use Fogwell's Gym to trigger Stick/Daredevil repeatedly
Billy Club and Elektra double as card engines
Stun Jessica Jones for a big card dig
Attack with Daredevil to exile and burn yourself

decks.description: Heroes for Hire is a mono-red Treasure-generation deck that ramps hard and converts extra mana and artifacts into card advantage and damage. Stark Industries Executive, Contract Hero, Luke Cage, and Heroes for Hire itself all pump out Treasure tokens, which feed Misty Knight's discard-loop card draw, K'un-Lun Warrior's ETB draw, and Iron Fist's expensive but explosive power-up that deals 5 damage divided among targets while adding five +1/+1 counters. Heroes for Hire's enchantment lets you sacrifice any of those Treasures to exile and play cards off the top of your library, effectively turning excess mana into card advantage on demand. Red Hulk's Enrage ability converts incoming damage into permanent +1/+1 counters and a damage ping, rewarding you for playing into combat, while Big Score and Bionic Blow give additional card draw and creature-based removal. The overall plan is to flood the board with Treasures early, use them to fix mana and dig for cards, then close with Iron Fist's or Red Hulk's damage output.
tips: Flood board with Treasures early
Sac Treasures via Heroes for Hire to dig
Use extra Treasures to power up Iron Fist
Red Hulk turns damage into counters and burn
Discard loop with Misty Knight for card draw

decks.description: Spider-Verse is a mono-red discard-matters deck where nearly every creature cares about you discarding cards, turning what would normally be a drawback into a repeatable value and damage engine. Spider-Man, New Champion deals damage equal to the number of cards discarded any time you discard, so pairing him with Astonishing Spider-Man's attack trigger (discard your hand, draw a card for each card discarded) or Daily Bugle Newspaper's loot ability can produce a burst of direct damage. Lady Spider, Maybelle Reilly and Hobgoblin, Mantled Marauder reward every discard with counters or a temporary power boost respectively, while Spider-Gwen, Free Spirit and Iceman and Firestar convert discarding into looting for card selection. Stegron the Dinosaur Man's Dinosaur Formula gives you a discard outlet that also pumps a creature, making it easy to keep the discard engine running turn after turn while snowballing damage and stats.
tips: Discard triggers Spider-Man's damage
Attack with Astonishing Spider-Man for card refill
Chain discards to grow Lady Spider's counters
Use Stegron as a discard outlet
Loot with Daily Bugle Newspaper repeatedly

decks.description: Vehicles is a mono-red artifact-crewing deck that builds around Vehicles entering the battlefield and being crewed for combat damage, with several payoffs scaling off how many Vehicles you control. Sky Cycle deals damage equal to twice your Vehicle count when it enters, so playing it after other Vehicles like Big Wheel or Captain America's Motorcycle land makes it a bigger removal spell, and Truck Toss costs {2} less whenever you control a Vehicle, turning it into cheap reach. The Thanos-Copter is the deck's centerpiece: it turns all your Vehicles into artifact creatures for the turn (letting you swing with everything at once without crewing) and then draws you a card whenever any Vehicle deals combat damage to a player, generating card advantage off a wide alpha strike. Machinesmith Automaton grows with every other artifact that enters, and HYDRA Assault Robot pings opponents off Villains and artifacts entering, giving the deck incidental reach alongside its Vehicle-crewing beatdown plan.
tips: Play multiple Vehicles before Sky Cycle
Truck Toss is cheap once a Vehicle is out
Thanos-Copter lets all Vehicles attack at once
Draw cards off Vehicle combat damage
Crew smartly, don't overcommit blockers

decks.description: Runaways is a mono-red graveyard/exile value deck that leans on casting spells from outside the hand to trigger its payoffs. Alex Wilder, Runaway gives +2/+0 and haste to any creature (including himself) that enters from somewhere other than hand, which combines directly with his own Escape ability, letting you exile three cards from the graveyard to recast him as a hasty beater. Nico Minoru, Runaway deals 2 damage to each opponent whenever you cast a spell from anywhere but your hand, and her activated ability exiles cards until hitting a nonland card you can cast for free, meaning every free cast off Nico also pings opponents thanks to her own trigger. Karolina Dean, Runaway ramps five colors of mana each turn (though it can't cast hand spells) to help pay for Nico and escape costs, while Victor Mancha, Chase Stein, and cards like Blazing Crescendo and Crossover Collaboration keep exiling and replaying cards to fuel the graveyard/exile engine.
tips: Escape Alex Wilder for a hasty attacker
Nico's free casts trigger her own damage
Use Karolina's mana for Nico/escape costs
Fill graveyard fast for Escape costs
Chain exile effects to keep card advantage

decks.description: Scarlet is a mono-red spells-matter deck centered on The Vision and Scarlet Witch, who adds a red mana and a +1/+1 counter to herself every time you cast a spell, letting you chain additional spells off the extra mana she generates while growing into a real threat. Loki Laufeyson can copy an upcoming cheap instant or sorcery for extra value, Wiccan, Young Avenger exiles and lets you play the top card of your library off each noncreature spell, and Wanda's Vision triggers on your second spell each turn to let you cast a free spell exiled from the top of your library, encouraging you to jam as many cheap spells as possible in a single turn. Molten Lavamancer converts any noncombat damage dealt to opponents into free 1/1 tokens, and Grapeshot's Storm ability scales with how many spells you've already cast that turn, making it a potential finisher once the deck has chained multiple cheap spells through Vision's mana and Wanda's Vision's free casts.
tips: Chain spells to grow Vision and Scarlet Witch
Cast second spell to trigger Wanda's Vision
Save Grapeshot for a big spell-heavy turn
Loki copies your best cheap spell
Molten Lavamancer farms tokens off burn

decks.description: This mono-green deck is built around a lifegain-to-counters engine, where every point of life gained snowballs into bigger creatures and more resources. Tigra, Feline Fury gets a +1/+1 counter whenever you gain life, and Heroic Feast doubles down by handing out a counter to up to that many target creatures whenever you gain life at all, meaning a single Food token sacrifice can pump your whole board. Tippy-Toe, Terrific Partner turns every token-making effect (like Ant-Man's Army's Food/Treasure choice or Lucky the Pizza Dog's Food generation off Cat/Dog/Hero spells) into an extra Food token plus a card draw at end step if you gained life that turn. Wakandan Tusker also gains life just from being tapped, feeding the engine passively. The deck isn't purely about racing; it grinds out incremental advantage through repeated small life gains that compound into permanent stat boosts, backed by removal like Guerrilla Gorilla's artifact/enchantment destruction and Scout the City's flexible mill-or-flying-removal mode.
tips: Sac Food tokens for counters
Chain lifegain into Heroic Feast triggers
Tippy-Toe doubles token value
Tap Wakandan Tusker for free lifegain
Save Guerrilla Gorilla for key artifacts

decks.description: This is a +1/+1 counter fight deck that wants to stack counters onto a few key threats and then use fight effects to clear blockers. Bushmaster, Coiled Henchman gives deathtouch to every other creature that already has a counter on it, so once creatures start picking up counters from Rhino, Terrible Trampler's ETB distribution or Claim the Kingdom's landfall trigger, they become efficient trading pieces in fights like Go Nuts! or Rhino's Rampage. Knight of Wundagore triggers off any other creature getting a counter (once per turn), snowballing its own size passively. Mister Hyde, Monster Within offers a choice engine, either growing himself or converting counters into card draw when you need gas instead of stats. Powerful Broker can double up any counter type on a permanent, further accelerating the counters plan. Overall it's a grindy, counter-centric midrange deck that leans on fighting removal rather than direct damage.
tips: Stack counters before fighting
Bushmaster grants deathtouch to countered creatures
Use Mister Hyde flexibly for cards or size
Claim the Kingdom rewards landfall
Double counters with Powerful Broker

decks.description: This deck is a ramp-into-big-power shell that cares specifically about creatures with power 4 or greater, using them to both draw cards and generate mana. Giant-Man, Gargantuan Genius adds green mana for each creature you control with power 4+, and Goliath, Mass Manipulator's power-up ability draws a card for each such creature, meaning once you have a couple of large bodies out, both ramp and card advantage explode. Atlas, Sizable Stooge similarly gains life for each power-4-plus creature whenever it attacks or blocks, tying the power theme into incidental life gain. Ms. Marvel, Elastic Ally and Ant-Man, Reformed Rogue reward pushing power above base stats or casting spells of certain colors to draw extra cards, while Reptil and Spider-Rex provide flexible large bodies to hit the power threshold. The deck plays out as a slower ramp deck that wants to establish a couple of size-4+ threats to unlock its payoffs, then close with fight spells like Colossal Collision and Terrific Team-Up.
tips: Get creatures to power 4+ fast
Giant-Man ramps off big bodies
Goliath draws cards per big creature
Use Terrific Team-Up as fight removal
Atlas gains life while defending

decks.description: This deck is a landfall and land-recursion engine that wants to flood the board with lands and tokens rather than just curve out normally. Ka-Zar of the Savage Land lets you play lands off the top of your library while creating Zabu, which grows every time a land enters, and Mole Man, Moloid Master lets you replay lands from the graveyard while spawning a 1/1 Moloid token on every landfall trigger. Claim the Kingdom also converts each land drop into a +1/+1 counter plus progress toward an indestructible counter payoff, and Gert and Old Lace can fetch an extra basic land straight to hand on ETB. Hellcat, Undying Vigilante provides a resilient recurring threat that returns bigger after dying, while Accelerated Evolution offers a combat trick that also protects a creature with hexproof. The overall plan is to jam as many lands into play as possible to trigger these landfall payoffs repeatedly, turning what looks like a ramp package into a wide, grindy value engine.
tips: Maximize land drops for landfall
Mole Man replays lands from graveyard
Zabu grows with every land
Claim the Kingdom rewards extra lands
Hellcat comes back bigger after dying

decks.description: This deck centers on The Unbeatable Squirrel Girl's token generation feeding into an explosive payoff: she creates a Squirrel on entry and on every attack, and her second ability, I LOVE Squirrels!, can be activated repeatedly to create X new Squirrels equal to your current Squirrel count, letting the token count double or more each activation if you have the mana. Tippy-Toe, Terrific Partner turns every one of those token-creation events into an extra Food token as well, and further rewards any lifegain that results with a card draw at end step. Mister Immortal offers cheap, repeatable recursion, coming back from graveyard or exile to rebuild the board after a wrath or combat losses. Training Regimen backs up the go-wide plan by handing out trample to countered creatures and adding a counter every combat, while Big Bertha scales directly with however much mana you invest into her enter-the-battlefield X cost.
tips: Attack with Squirrel Girl to snowball tokens
I LOVE Squirrels! doubles your board
Tippy-Toe adds Food to every token trigger
Mister Immortal recurs cheaply
Training Regimen grants trample to counters

decks.description: This is a Gamma-creature +1/+1 counter doubling deck built around explosive exponential growth. Hulk, Strongest There Is doubles the number of counters on each Gamma creature you control every upkeep, and Doc Samson, Super Psychiatrist adds an extra counter of each kind whenever counters would be placed, so a single Origin of the Hulk chapter II trigger or Go Nuts! activation snowballs fast once both are online. She-Hulk, Attorney-at-Law's power-up doubles counters across your whole board on top of that, meaning stacking Doc Samson, Hulk, and She-Hulk together can turn a handful of counters into a lethal army in a single turn cycle. Brawn, Amadeus Cho draws cards on entry and later converts your whole hand into a massive one-time counter payload. The deck plays a slower buildup early, using Guerrilla Gorilla and Hulk's Thunderclap for removal, before unleashing a huge doubling turn to close the game.
tips: Stack Doc Samson before adding counters
Hulk doubles Gamma counters each upkeep
She-Hulk power-up doubles whole board
Use Brawn's power-up with a full hand
Hold removal for key blockers

decks.description: This deck is built entirely around cheap 'power-up' abilities that scale down in cost the turn a creature enters, then reward stacking +1/+1 counters across the board. Shang-Chi, Martial Mentor doubles every +1/+1 counter placed on your creatures, so activating Wakandan Royal Guard's ETB (which already gives two counters to Heroes) or any power-up ability after Shang-Chi resolves effectively doubles its value. Advancing the Spirit lets you activate one power-up ability each turn for free, letting you discount and then completely waive the cost of your biggest power-up creature, such as She-Hulk's counter-doubling ability or Hercules's indestructible/haste grant. Punishing Punch and Colossal Collision turn all those pumped-up creatures into efficient removal by dealing damage equal to power (or twice power) to an opponent's creature. The overall plan is an incremental value-and-size engine that gets sharply better the more power-up abilities you chain together in a single turn.
tips: Play Shang-Chi before adding counters
Advancing the Spirit frees a power-up
Chain multiple power-ups per turn
Punishing Punch scales with power
Wakandan Royal Guard favors Heroes

decks.description: This is a tribal Mutant deck that rewards playing Mutants into each other for compounding stat growth and combat math advantage. X-23, Deadly Weapon puts a +1/+1 counter on both herself and any other Mutant that enters, Colossus, Steel Stalwart buffs all other Mutants by +1/+1 passively, and Wolverine, Claws Out doubles the power of any attacking Mutant, turning even modest counter growth into huge damage swings once Wolverine and a buffed Mutant attack together. Darwin, Adaptive Mutant grows via Evolve whenever a bigger creature enters and can spend counters for emergency indestructibility, while Beast, Erudite Aerialist gains flying and draws cards whenever he's been pumped with counters that turn. Training Regimen adds trample to any countered creature, letting the deck's growing Mutants actually punch through blockers instead of just getting bigger for no purpose.
tips: Sequence Mutants for X-23 counters
Wolverine doubles attacking Mutant power
Buff before attacking with Mutants
Darwin trades counters for indestructible
Training Regimen adds trample

decks.description: This three-color-adjacent (mono-green splash-enabled via Savage Lands) deck is a Dinosaur tribal build focused on turning any creature into a Dinosaur and then protecting and pumping the tribe. Sauron, Dino Devotee's saurian counter ability can turn any target creature into a 5/5 green Dinosaur, instantly qualifying it for Devil Dinosaur's blanket +1/+1 and hexproof buff to all other Dinosaurs, and for Moon-Boy, Dino Rider's cost reduction and attack trigger, which only fires while you control a Dinosaur. Ka-Zar of the Savage Land plays lands off the top of the library while making Zabu, a token that grows with every landfall, feeding the ramp needed for Reptil's expensive mode-shifting abilities. Lurking Lizards passively grows off any expensive spell you cast, rewarding the deck's naturally high curve, while Commune with Dinosaurs digs specifically for more Dinosaurs or lands to keep the plan rolling.
tips: Sauron converts any creature to Dinosaur
Devil Dinosaur buffs and protects tribe
Ka-Zar plus Zabu ramps landfall
Cast big spells to grow Lurking Lizards
Commune with Dinosaurs finds pieces

decks.description: This is a graveyard-matters green deck that wants creatures dying and cards milling into the yard to fuel its payoffs. Voracious Brood enters with a +1/+1 counter for every creature card already in your graveyard and keeps growing every time more creatures die anywhere, making it a scaling late-game threat if you've been trading creatures away. Punishing Punch gets cheaper and hits harder once two or more creature cards are in your graveyard, turning cheap fights into big removal. Hellcat, Undying Vigilante and White Tiger, Amulet Keeper both offer graveyard recursion or exile-for-value effects, letting you grind resources back even after creatures die. Return of the Mole Man and Scout the City add self-mill to feed the graveyard count, while Call Damage Control can retrieve almost any permanent type back to hand once it's there, making this deck resilient to attrition and rewarding a slower, grindier game plan.
tips: Mill early to fuel Voracious Brood
Punishing Punch cheaper with dead creatures
Let creatures trade to grow payoffs
Return of the Mole Man mills for tokens
Call Damage Control rebuys key permanents

decks.description: This four-color Fantastic Four deck is built around card draw and +1/+1 counter synergies that snowball into big damage and defensive Wall tokens. Mister Fantastic, Reed Richards draws you a card whenever tokens enter, which is fed directly by Invisible Woman, Sue Storm's ability to create a 0/4 Wall token whenever you put counters on other Heroes, and by The Fantastic Four's modal ability which can also make Walls. Human Torch, Johnny Storm punishes that same card-draw engine by pinging opponents for 1 damage every time you draw, so a single Mister Fantastic trigger can chain into extra draws and extra pings. The Thing, Ben Grimm grows permanently whenever Heroes deal combat damage, turning him into a late-game trampling threat, while spells like Inspired Fire and Daily Bugle Newspaper get cheaper or more efficient the more you're drawing and discarding that turn. Thing Swing lets a beefed-up Hero (especially a counter-loaded The Thing) snipe an opposing creature by dealing double its power as damage, rewarding you for growing your creatures first. The deck ramps with Farseek, Terramorphic Expanse, and multicolor lands like Baxter Building and Pym Technologies to smooth out its four-color mana base while accumulating incremental card advantage and defensive bodies until it can close with The Fantastic Four's direct damage mode or a huge Thing attack.
tips: Chain draws off Mister Fantastic
Use Sue Storm for free Walls
Grow The Thing before attacking
Combo Thing Swing with pumped Heroes
Ramp mana with Farseek/Terramorphic first
